architectureVision.statement: Enable Arrive through an integrated enterprise architecture spanning business, application, data, and technology domains.
drivers: Reduce fragmentation across organizational and technology change.
Make dependencies and ownership explicit across architecture layers.
Improve governance of strategic change and target-state decisions.

goals.name: 2026-Q1 Urban Mobility Policy Director outcome goal
goals.description: Improve Increase share of city decisions backed by real mobility data for Urban Mobility Policy Director by advancing credible policy-grade analytics and low-friction integration through mobility intelligence core.

goals.name: 2026-Q1 Transit Revenue and Operations Executive outcome goal
goals.description: Improve Increase share of transit rides with low-friction digital payment for Transit Revenue and Operations Executive by advancing operational reliability and payment modernization through open payment transit core.

goals.name: 2026-Q2 Urban Mobility Policy Director outcome goal
goals.description: Prepare to improve Increase share of city decisions backed by real mobility data for Urban Mobility Policy Director by advancing credible policy-grade analytics and low-friction integration through mobility intelligence core.

goals.name: 2026-Q2 Transit Revenue and Operations Executive outcome goal
goals.description: Prepare to improve Increase share of transit rides with low-friction digital payment for Transit Revenue and Operations Executive by advancing operational reliability and payment modernization through open payment transit core.

principles: Business capabilities drive application and technology design.
Information is modeled as a shared enterprise asset.
Technology choices should be standardized unless a domain-specific need justifies variation.
Implementation should be governed against explicit target and transition architectures.

stakeholders.name: Municipal Parking Director
stakeholders.concern: Leader responsible for city-owned curbside and off-street parking performance. Strategic accountability: parking revenue, utilization, compliance, and resident-driver experience. Economic driver: net parking yield per space and collection efficiency. Risk exposure: revenue leakage, failed digital rollout, political backlash from poor user experience, or non-compliance with payment and EV standards.

stakeholders.name: Urban Mobility Policy Director
stakeholders.concern: Public-sector decision-maker accountable for congestion, livability, sustainability, modal shift, and transport demand management. Strategic accountability: achieve policy goals while maintaining public trust and operational feasibility. Economic driver: cost-effective movement of people and reduction of congestion-related externalities. Risk exposure: failed policy outcomes, poor adoption of city mobility services, underused infrastructure, and political scrutiny from ineffective programs.

stakeholders.name: Transit Revenue and Operations Executive
stakeholders.concern: Executive responsible for fare collection, boarding efficiency, ticketing infrastructure, operational reliability, and customer access across buses, trams, and rail. Strategic accountability: grow ridership while controlling operating complexity and collection cost. Economic driver: revenue collected per rider at acceptable cost and service reliability. Risk exposure: boarding friction, outage-prone systems, fare leakage, failed upgrades, or inability to support modern open payments.

stakeholders.name: Fleet Mobility and Expense Manager
stakeholders.concern: Operational leader managing vehicle mobility, field-team parking, visitor parking, and reimbursement control across small businesses, enterprises, rental operators, or service fleets. Strategic accountability: reduce parking friction and expense leakage while keeping teams productive. Economic driver: productive time recovered and parking cost controlled per employee or vehicle. Risk exposure: uncontrolled spend, reimbursement chaos, field-team downtime, and poor employee experience.

stakeholders.name: Mobility Platform Partner Manager
stakeholders.concern: Commercial or product leader at a parking app, charge point operator, payment partner, or local mobility provider that wants access to Arrive-managed demand, inventory, or infrastructure. Strategic accountability: grow transaction volume and market reach through ecosystem participation. Economic driver: partner-side GMV, active users, and margin after integration cost. Risk exposure: being locked out of key cities, poor economics under closed models, or fragmented integration work across markets.
